commit_msg: refactor(pipeline): simplify async event loop handling
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Architect Unmakes Himself
subtitle: Midday on main, he tore out the guts of the machine and put it back together backwards.
when_where: 🕐 12:19 PM, main branch, the hour when the city stops pretending to work. The async event loop had been running like a corrupt bureaucrat for months—layers of delegation, chains of command, nobody knowing who was really in charge. 🏙️ Omkar sat with the codebase spread across three monitors like a corpse on a slab.
who_whom: 🔍 The pipeline was acting on itself, a recursive nightmare of callbacks stacked on promises stacked on coroutines. He was the only one who could see how deep the rot went. 💀 The refactor wasn't mercy—it was an amputation. He was simplifying by erasure, which meant something had to die for the machine to breathe again.
what_why: ⚙️ He stripped the async event loop to its skeleton, ripped out the ornamental complexity, the defensive layers that had accumulated like scar tissue. Every line he deleted was a confession that the old architecture was a lie. 🔥 The pipeline ran cleaner now, but only because he'd removed everything that wasn't essential—including, maybe, the parts that kept him sane. 🧠 Simplicity looks like intelligence until you realize it's just desperation dressed up in minimalism.
verdict: He didn't refactor the code; he refactored his own tolerance for chaos.
